mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals a decisive labor market cooling that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based aggregate demand growth that sustains corporate revenue momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield a 60-day forward return hit rate below 50%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum predicts short-term equity strength, but this relationship only holds during late-cycle expansions where capacity utilization remains elevated and supply chains operate efficiently.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in > 65% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when the regime filter is applied.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the threshold effect shows no incremental predictive power over a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll growth reflects robust labor market conditions that support wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales indicates sector-specific demand imbalances that trigger short-term portfolio rebalancing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a 5-day hit rate below 45%, or the cross-series divergence shows no statistically significant relationship with short-term equity direction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth momentum predicts positive equity returns, but this mechanism only activates during monetary tightening cycles where strong growth offsets higher discount rates and supports earnings resilience.
failure_condition: GDP momentum signals produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of observations during tightening periods, or the regime-conditional coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.05).
